data.title: Jericho Energy announces COO appointment
data.description:
• Jericho Energy Ventures (JEV:CA) promoted Jake Royster to chief operating officer from his prior role as director of operations.
  • Royster's achievements include lowest per-barrel lifting costs in over eight quarters and maintaining profitability despite a 17% price decline.
  • He also improved operational efficiency, communication processes, and safety standards across field operations.

data.title: Vertical Aerospace shares set record low, reiterates "going concern” warning -- update
data.description:
Updated 3/27 17:00 ET with a statement from a Vertical Aerospace spokesperson regarding the company's financial performance and capital position.

Shares of Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) are under pressure on Tuesday and dipped to a record low after the company reiterated that it has “substantial doubt it can continue to conduct its normal business operations in the foreseeable future” without liquidating assets or restructuring its obligations. This is a statement the company has repeated since 2023. 

“Our limited cash and cash equivalents, recurring losses from operations, and dependency on raising additional capital indicate that a material uncertainty exists that may cast significant doubt regarding our ability to continue as a going concern,” the company said in a  filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

For 2025, Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) reported a net profit of £202.6M ($271.6M) for 2025, or £2.63 per share, but a loss of £0.57 per share ($0.76) on a diluted basis, missing expectations for a loss of £0.0075 ($0.01). Last year, Vertical (EVTL) reported a loss of £38.46 per diluted share.

On its balance sheet, Vertical (EVTL) raised $175M in capital, ended the year with $93M in cash and cash equivalents, and anticipates approximately $23M in near-term receipts from R&D tax reliefs and another $5M in government grants and VAT. At the same time, Vertical (EVTL) expects to spend $195M in flight testing, certification, and manufacturing, an outflow that must be covered through either government grants, R&D tax relief, or additional funding. 

Operationally, Vertical (EVTL) is making solid progress towards its goal of achieving certification and scaling production of its electric vertical take-off and landing ((eVTOL)) aircraft. This includes the launch of Valo, its commercial eVTOL aircraft designed for 4-6 passengers, its battery pilot program line, and completed a third prototype to provide increased flight capacity.

It also completed hover, vertical flight, and wingborne flight, including the first winged eVTOL flight in open European airspace and airport-to-airport flight at the Royal International Air Tattoo.

However, despite operational achievements, Vertical (EVTL) cautioned that since the market for its offerings is still in the relatively early stage of growth, if these markets do not continue to grow, grow more slowly than expected, or fail to grow as large as expected, the company’s business, financial condition, and results of operations could be harmed.

“If we are not able to overcome these barriers, our business, prospects, operating results, and financial condition will be negatively impacted, and our ability to grow our business will be harmed,” Vertical said in its SEC filing.

Nonetheless, Vertical (EVTL) CEO Stuart Simpson remains optimistic as the company is advancing its plans to expand at Cotswold Airport. 

“We’ve made strong progress across the business—achieving key piloted flight milestones, advancing transition testing, and launching Valo to the market. The response from customers and partners has been very encouraging, with renewed order activity and growing confidence in our program. We are now firmly focused on certification and scaling production.”

Statement from Vertical Aerospace spokesperson: As disclosed in the Annual Report and discussed on the Q4 earnings call, we’ve made strong progress across our flight test program, manufacturing, and commercial development over the past twelve months and are closer than ever to certification and entry into service. Vertical’s anticipated short-term liquidity totals approximately $85 million.

The language cited in our Annual Report is a standard accounting disclosure for a company at Vertical’s stage of development and has appeared consistently in the company’s filings since 2023. It does not reflect any new development.

data.title: Instacart rips NYC delivery laws, will require 'fundamental' change to how app operates
data.description:
As a result of new grocery delivery laws in New York City that require apps to pay an hourly minimum for any time shoppers are on the app, Instacart (CART) is introducing changes to how the platform operates, including a new acceptance rate metric.

Beginning January 26, New York City now requires third-party apps, including Instacart (CART), DoorDash (DASH), and Uber Eats (UBER) to pay delivery workers a minimum of $21.44 per hour—excluding tips—for the total time workers are logged into a platform’s app. To compensate for this increased cost, Instacart (CART) introduced a $5.99 regulatory response fee to consumers last month.

But in a post sharply critical of the new law, Instacart (CART) said the new law will require more fundamental changes to how the app operates, especially as delivery workers—or “shoppers,” as they are referred to by Instacart—are to be paid for time they are simply logged into the app.

“That change forces a bigger shift: if any shopper can go online whenever they want, the platform would be required to pay for large amounts of idle time that may not correspond to actual work or real-time demand. Under that model, the app simply cannot operate as it has,” Instacart said on their website, adding that the city has left the company “with only one viable option: limit how many shoppers can be online at any given time.”

As a result, beginning April 1, Instacart (CART) will now impose constraints on when shoppers can go online based on market dynamics.

Shoppers now have a limited time to accept each offer and accept one-at-a-time batch offers instead of an available batch list. Additionally, the company implemented a new acceptance rate metric.

“Despite what shoppers have said they value, the city moved forward with a one-size-fits-all framework without meaningful engagement with grocery delivery workers or a serious effort to understand how grocery delivery differs from restaurant delivery," the company added. 

For customers, the changes could lead to increased fees, longer delivery times (as shoppers are now limited to one order at a time), and smaller batches more frequently rejected. Accordingly, Instacart (CART) shares slid into the red in the final hour of trading.

data.title: MongoDB is the worst performing large-cap IT stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap information technology stocks are in a consolidation phase year-to-date, with the S&P 500 Information Technology sector down about 4% in 2026 through March after a blockbuster 24% gain in 2025.

Below is a list of the 10 worst performing large-cap IT stocks with market capitalizations of $10 billion or more. The list is arranged by their year-to-date (YTD) performance decline.

The list is topped by MongoDB, Inc. (MDB), with a YTD performance down 42.51%. AppLovin Corporation (APP) and Figma, Inc. (FIG) follow closely behind, with Workday, Inc. (WDAY) and HubSpot, Inc. (HUBS) rounding out the rest of the bottom five.

Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO), Gartner, Inc. (IT), and Zscaler, Inc. (ZS) are among the other notable underperformers. Intuit Inc. (INTU) and GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY) complete the list of the ten worst performers in the large-cap IT space.

Here is the list:

  1. MongoDB, Inc. (MDB), YTD perf: -42.51%, Quant Rating: Buy 4.07

  2. AppLovin Corporation (APP), YTD perf: -41.94%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.24

  3. Figma, Inc. (FIG), YTD perf: -40.73%, Quant Rating: Not Covered

data.title: Freddie Mac is the worst performing large-cap financial stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap financial stocks have had a weak start to 2026, with the S&P 500 Financials sector down roughly 8% year-to-date through mid‑March, making it one of the worst-performing major sectors despite relatively solid fundamentals.

Below is a list of the 10 worst-performing large-cap financial stocks with market capitalizations over $14 billion, ranked by their year-to-date performance.

The list is led by Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (FMCC) and Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA), both mortgage finance giants that have seen declines exceeding 50% YTD. Affirm Holdings, Inc. (AFRM), a payment processing company, rounds out the top three worst performers with a decline of over 41%.

Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL), SoFi Technologies, Inc. (SOFI), and Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) also appear among the significant laggards, each down more than 37% YTD. Major asset management firms make up the remainder of the bottom ten, including TPG Inc. (TPG), Ares Management Corporation (ARES), KKR & Co. Inc. (KKR), and Blackstone Inc. (BX).

Here is the list:

  1. Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (FMCC), YTD perf: -54.64%, Quant rating: 3.08

data.title: Kalshi to offer margin trading that lets users open positions without putting up full capital - report
data.description:
Prediction market platform Kalshi (KALSHI) is said to have secured a license to offer margin trading to users through an affiliate called Kinetic Markets.

Margin trading is expected to allow users to open positions without putting up the full amount of capital, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing a filing with the National Futures Association.

The prediction market is not expected to launch margin on event contracts immediately, a person familiar with the matter told Bloomberg, adding that it could be made available sooner for other types of products.

The new product will only be available to institutions initially, according to the sources.

Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour reportedly said during a panel discussion moderated by Bloomberg that a margin trading product would be coming soon, and it was their priority to make the platform more capital-efficient for institutional investors.

data.title: Rubio confirms U.S. may divert Ukraine-bound weapons to Middle East
data.description:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters the U.S. hasn't ruled out diverting weapons earmarked for Ukraine to the Iran war effort.

“It hasn’t happened yet. Nothing yet has been diverted, but it could,” Rubio told reporters after a Group of Seven ministers meeting on Friday, according to Bloomberg.

Rubio noted the weapons in question are supplied through the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program used by European NATO members to buy U.S.-made weapons for the Ukrainian military.

“These are military sales through PURL paid for by NATO,” Rubio said. "These are our weapons. These are sales.”

On Thursday, the Washington Post reported that the Pentagon was considering diverting certain weapon shipments from Ukraine to the Middle East, where munition stockpiles are running low due to the war with Iran. 

“Let me be clear: If the United States has a military need, whether it is to replenish our stocks or to fulfill some mission in the national interest of the United States, we’re always going to come first when it comes to our weapons,” Rubio said, according to Bloomberg, adding that PURL has been “unaffected by this operation.”

data.title: BiomX receives NYSE American non-compliance notice
data.description:
• BiomX (PHGE) received a notice from NYSE American LLC for failing to meet minimum stockholders’ equity requirements.
  • The deficiency relates to multiple thresholds ($2M, $4M, $6M) tied to recurring net losses under listing standards.
  • BiomX must submit a compliance plan by April 24, 2026, to regain compliance by September 25, 2027.
  • If the plan is accepted, the company will undergo periodic and quarterly monitoring by NYSE American.
  • Failure to submit or execute the plan could result in delisting proceedings, though appeals are permitted.
  • The notice has no immediate impact, and shares will continue trading.
  • The company previously disclosed a going concern audit opinion in its 2025 annual report.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Monday
data.description:
U.S. equities closed out Friday in negative territory, as all three major indexes were under pressure as President Donald Trump prolonged the suspension of strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure through April, adding to geopolitical uncertainty.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) declined 2.1%, while the broader S&P 500 (SP500) fell 1.6%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) also moved lower, shedding 1.7% as selling pressure remained widespread across sectors.

Now, here are three focus points for investors on Monday:

  • Traders will be closely watching Monday’s release of the Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index, as investors look for fresh signals on regional business activity and the broader health of the U.S. industrial sector. The report will be scrutinized for changes in production, new orders, and pricing pressures, which could offer insight into manufacturing momentum and help shape expectations around economic growth and the Federal Reserve’s policy path.
  • Investors will also be monitoring developments in the ongoing Middle East conflict between the U.S. and Iran, as geopolitical risk remains a key driver of market sentiment. Comments from Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicating the conflict could persist for another two to four weeks have reinforced expectations that a resolution may be approaching, while still leaving room for near-term volatility.
  • Market participants will also be keeping a close eye on the Treasury market, as yields climbed to multi-month highs on Friday, drawing increased attention to fixed-income volatility. The move higher in rates could continue to influence equity valuations and broader risk appetite across financial markets.
